content: Which one of the following reactions does NOT belong to "Lassaigne's test"?
explanation:
Nitrogen, sulphur, halogens and phosphorus present in an organic compound are detected by "Lassaigne's test".

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{C}+\mathrm{N} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{NaCN} \\ & 2 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{S} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S} \\ & \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{X} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underset{(\mathrm{X}=\mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{Br}, \mathrm{l})}{\mathrm{NaX}} \end{aligned}$$

content:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Lithium and beryllium unlike their other respective group members form compounds with pronounced ionic character.

Reason (R) : Lithium and Magnesium have similar properties due to diagonal relationship.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation: Li, Be forms predominately covalent compounds.

content:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion $$\mathbf{A}$$ and the other is labelled as Reason $$\mathbf{R}$$ :

Assertion A : Metallic sodium dissolves in liquid ammonia giving a deep blue solution, which is paramagnetic.

Reason R : The deep blue solution is due to the formation of amide.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :

options.content: Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are true but $$\mathbf{R}$$ is NOT the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$.
Both $$\mathbf{A}$$ and $$\mathbf{R}$$ are true and $$\mathbf{R}$$ is the correct explanation of $$\mathbf{A}$$.

explanation:
Assertion is correct because all Alkali metals gives deep blue solution by giving electrons.

Reason is incorrect because deep blue solution appears due to the presence of ammoniated electron or solvated electrons.

content: Which one of the following statements is correct?

options.content: All enzymes that utilise ATP in phosphate transfer require $$\mathrm{Ca}$$ as the cofactor.
The bone in human body is an inert and unchanging substance.
$$\mathrm{Mg}$$ plays roles in neuromuscular function and interneuronal transmission.
The daily requirement of $$\mathrm{Mg}$$ and $$\mathrm{Ca}$$ in the human body is estimated to be 0.2 - 0.3 $$\mathrm{g}$$.

explanation: The daily requirement in the human body has been estimated to be 200-300 mg (NCERT : s-block) Biological importance of magnesium and calcium.

content: Weight (g) of two moles of the organic compound, which is obtained by heating sodium ethanoate with sodium hydroxide in presence of calcium oxide is :
explanation: Weight $$=2\times16=32$$ g

content: The element used for welding metals with high melting points is
explanation: Atomic hydrogen atoms produced by dissociation of dihydrogen with the help of electric arc use for cutting and welding metals with high melting points.

content: CaCl2 and Ca(OCl)2 are components of
explanation:
Milk of lime reacts with chlorine to form hypochlorite, a constituent of bleaching powder

2Ca(OH)2 + 2Cl2 $$\to$$ $$\mathop {\mathrm{CaC{l_2} + Ca{{(OCl)}_2}}}\limits_{\mathrm{Bleaching\,Powder}} $$ + 2H2O

content: Which one of the following is not a calcination reaction?
explanation:
Calcination involves heating when the volatile matter escapes leaving behind the metal oxide.

Fe2O3 . xH2O(s) $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ Fe2O3(s) + xH2O(g)

ZnCO3(s) $$\to$$ ZnO(s) + CO2(g)

CaCO3 . MgCO3(s) $$\to$$ CaO(s) + MgO(s) + 2CO2(g)

CaCO3 + 2HCl $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ CaCl2 + H2O + CO2 Not calcination

It is a neutralization reaction.

content: Identify the incorrect statement from the following

options.content: Alkali metals react with water to form their hydroxides.
The oxidation number of K in KO<sub>2</sub> is +4.
Ionisation enthalpy of alkali metals decreases from top to bottom in the group.
Lithium is the strongest reducing agent among the alkali metals.

explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Alkali metals show only '+1' oxidation state in all of their compounds.

KO2 is a super-oxide in which O$$_2^ - $$ is anion and K+ is cation oxidation state of K is +1.

content:
Match List - I with List - II

List - I List - II
(a) Li (i) absorbent for carbon dioxide
(b) Na (ii) electrochemical cells
(c) KOH (iii) coolant in fast breeder reactors
(d) Cs (iv) photoelectric cell

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Cs is used in photoelectric cell due to its low ionisation enthalpy

$$\bullet$$ KOH used to absorb CO2 and changes into K2CO3

$$\bullet$$ Liquid sodium metal is used as a coolant in fast breeder nuclear reactor

$$\bullet$$ Lithium is used in electrochemical cells

content: Among the following alkaline earth metal halides, one which is covalent and soluble in organic solvents is :
explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Except for beryllium chloride all other chloride of alkaline earth metals are ionic in nature.

$$\bullet$$ Due to small size of Be, Beryllium chloride is essentially covalent and soluble in organic solvents.

content: The structures of beryllium chloride in solid state and vapour phase, are :
explanation:
Beryllium chloride has a chain structure in the solid state as shown below


In vapour phase Beryllium chloride tends to form a chloro-bridged dimer.

content: The following metal ion activities many enzymes, participates in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP and with Na, is responsible for the transmission of nerve signals.
explanation: Potassium (K) activities many enzymes participate in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP and helps in the transmission of nerve signal along with Na.

content: HCl was passed through A solution of CaCl<sub>2</sub>, MgCl<sub>2</sub> and NaCl. Which of the following compound(s) crystallize(s)?
explanation: Since CaCl<sub>2</sub> and MgCl<sub>2</sub> are more soluble than NaCl, on passing HCl(g) through A solution containing CaCl<sub>2</sub>, MgCl<sub>2</sub> and NaCl, then NaCl crystallizes out (common ion effect).

content: Which of the following is an amphoteric hydroxide?
explanation:
Be(OH)2 is an amphoteric hydroxide as it reacts with acid as well as base while others are basic hydroxide.

Be(OH)2 + 2HCl $$ \to $$ BeCl2 + 2H2O

Be(OH)2 + 2NaOH $$ \to $$ Na2[Be(OH)4]

content: Enzymes that utilize ATP is phosphate transfer require an alkaline earth metal (M) as the cofactor. M is
explanation:
Mg is acts as a cofactor for all the enzymes
involved in ATP phosphate transfer.

content:
Among CaH<sub>2</sub>
, BeH<sub>2</sub>
, BaH<sub>2</sub>
, the order of ionic
character is
explanation:
Smaller the size of cation, more will be its polarising power. Hence BeH2 will be least ionic.

BeH2 < CaH2 < BaH2

content:
Magnesium reacts with an element (X) to form
an ionic compound. If the ground state
electronic configuration of (X) is 1s<sup>2</sup>2s<sup>2</sup>2p<sup>3</sup>,
the simplest formula for this compound is
explanation:
Electronic configuration of element (X) is
1s22s22p3

So, valency of X will be 3.
Since, valency of Mg is 2.
So, formula of compound formed by Mg and X will be Mg3X2.

content: Ionic mobility of which of the following alkali metal ions is lowest when aqueous solution of their salts are put under an electric field?
explanation:
Li+ being smallest, has maximum charge density.

So, Li+ is most heavily hydrated among all alkali metal ions. Effective size of Li+ in aqueous solution is therefore, largest. So, moves slowest under electric field.

content: The suspension of slaked lime in water is known as
explanation:
This process is known as slaking of lime.

The paste of lime in water (suspension) is called milk of lime while the filtered and clear solution is known as lime water.

content: In context with beryllium, which one of the following statements is incorrect?

options.content: It is rendered passive by nitric acid.
Its hydride is electron-deficient and polymeric.

explanation: Beryllium salts are readily hydrolysed.

content: Which of the following statments is false?

options.content: Ca<sup>2+</sup> ions are not important in maintaining the regular beating of the heart.
Mg<sup>2+</sup> ions are important in the green parts of the plants.
Mg<sup>2+</sup> ions form a complex with ATP.
Ca<sup>2+</sup> ions are important in blood clotting.

explanation:
Ca<sup>2+</sup> ions are required to trigger the
contraction of muscles and to maintain the regular
beating of the heart.

content: The product obtained as a result of a reaction of nitrogen with CaC<sub>2</sub> is
explanation: CaC<sub>2</sub> + N<sub>2</sub> $$ \to $$ CaCN<sub>2</sub> + C

content:
20.0 g of a magnesium carbonate sample decomposes on heating to give carbon dioxide and 8.0 g magnesium oxide. What will be the percentage purity of magnesium carbonate in the sample?
(At. wt. of Mg = 24)
explanation:
MgCO3 $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ MgO + CO2

84 g of MgCO3 $$ \equiv $$ 40 g of MgO

$$ \therefore $$ 20 g of MgCO3 = $${{40} \over {84}} \times 20$$ = 9.52 g of MgO

But Actual yield = 8 g of MgO

$$ \therefore $$ % purity = $${8 \over {9.52}} \times 100$$ = 84%

content: On heating which of the following releases CO<sub>2</sub> most easily?
explanation:
Carbonates becomes more thermally stable down the group, Also, the alkali metal carbonate are more stable than alkaline earth metal carbonates. Therefore MgCO3 will leave CO2 easily.

MgCO3 $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ MgO + CO2

content: Solubility of the alkaline earth metal sulphates in water decreases in the sequence
explanation:
Solubility of alkaline earth metal sulphates
decreases down the group because hydration
energy decreases.

content: The function of ''Sodium pump'' is a biological process operating in each and every cell of all animals. Which of the following biologically important ions is also a constituent of this pump?
explanation: K<sup>+</sup> ion is a constituent of sodium pump.

content: In Castner-Kellner cell for production of sodium hydroxide

options.content: brine is electrolyzed using graphite electrodes
sodium amalgam is formed at mercury cathode
brine is electrolyzed with Pt electrodes

explanation:
In Castner-Kellner cell, sodium amalgam is
formed at mercury cathode.

content: The ease of adsorption of the hydrated alkali metal ions on an ion-exchange resins follows the order :
explanation:
All alkali metal salts are ionic (except Lithium) and soluble in water due to the fact that cations get hydrated by water molecules. The degree of hydration depends upon the size of the cation. Smaller the size of a cation, greater is its hydration energy.

Relative ionic radii :

Li+ < K+ < Na+ < Rb+

Relative ionic radii in water or relative degree of hydration:

Rb+ < K+ < Na+ < Li+

content: Which one of the alkali metals, forms only, the normal oxide, M<sub>2</sub>O on heating in air?
explanation:
When alkali metals are heated in atmosphere
of oxygen the alkali metals ignite and form oxides.
On combustion, Li forms Li<sub>2</sub>O, sodium gives the
peroxide Na<sub>2</sub>O<sub>2</sub> while K and Rb give super oxides
(MO<sub>2</sub>).

content:
Match List-I with List-II for the compositions of substances and select the correct answer using the code given above.

List-I
(Substances)		 List-II
(Composition)
(A) Plaster of Pairs (i) CaSO4$$ \cdot $$2H2O
(B) Epsomite (ii) CaSO4$$ \cdot $$1/2 H2O
(C) Kieserite (iii) MgSO4$$ \cdot $$7H2O
(D) Gypsum (iv) MgSO4$$ \cdot $$H2O
(v) CaSO4
explanation:
(A) Plaster of paris = CaSO4.$${1 \over 2}$$H2O

(B) Epsomite = MgSO4.7H2O

(C) Kieserite = MgSO4.H2O

(D) Gypsum = CaSO4.2H2O

content: Which one of the following is present as an active ingredient in bleaching powder for bleaching action?
explanation:
Active ingredient in bleaching powder
for bleaching action is Ca(OCl)<sub>2</sub>, i.e., calcium
hypochlorite.

content: Which of the following compounds has the lowest melting point?
explanation:
As the covalent character in compound
increases and ionic character decreases, melting
point of the compound decreases. So, CaI<sub>2</sub> has the
highest covalent character and lowest melting point.

content: The compound A on heating gives a colourless gas and a residue that is dissolved in water to obtain B. Excess of CO<sub>2</sub> is bubbled through aqueous solution of B, C is formed which is recovered in the solid form. Solid C on gentle heating gives back A. The compound is
explanation:
A $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ colourless gas + residue

Residue + H2O $$ \to $$ B $$\buildrel {excess\,\,C{O_2}} \over \longrightarrow $$ C $$\buildrel \Delta \over \longrightarrow $$ A

This is possible only when ‘A’ is CaCO3. The reactions are as follow :

content: Which one of the following compounds is a peroxide?
Which of the following alkaline earth metal sulphates has hydration enthalpy higher than the lattice enthalpy?
explanation:
The hydration enthalpy of BeSO<sub>4</sub>
 is higher
than its lattice energy. Within group 2, the hydration
energy decreases down the group while lattice
energy is almost the same.

content: Property of the alkaline earth metals that increases with their atomic number

options.content: solubility of their hydroxides in water
solubility of their sulphates in water

explanation:
On moving down the group, basic nature of
these hydroxides increases due to low ionization
enthalpies because M-O bond in MOH is weak and
thus break to give OH<sup>–</sup> ions in the solution.

content: In the case of alkali metals, the covalent character decreases in the order
explanation:
According to Fajans’ rule,

Covalent character $$ \propto $$ size of anion $$ \propto $$
1
size of cation
.

Now the order of size of anions in the given options is as follows

F– < Cl– < Br– < I–

Thus, the covalent character of the given compounds varies as

MI > MBr > MCl > MF

content: Which of the following oxides is not expected to react with sodium hydroxide?
explanation:
NaOH is a strong alkali. It combines with
acidic and amphoteric oxides to form salts. Since
CaO is a basic oxide hence does not reacts with
NaOH.

content: The alkali metals form salt-like hydrides by the direct synthesis at elevated temperature. The thermal stability of these hydrides decreases in which of the following orders?
explanation:
The stability of alkali metal hydrides decreases from Li to Cs. It is due to the fact that M–H bonds becomes weaker with increase in size of alkali metals as we move down the group from Li to Cs. Thus the order of stability of hydrides is

LiH > NaH > KH > RbH > CsH

content: Equimolar solutions of the following were prepared in water separately. Which one of the solutions will record the highest pH?
explanation:
Equimolar solutions of the given chlorides when prepared in water forms their respective hydroxides.

SrCl2 + 2H2O $$ \to $$ Sr(OH)2 + 2HCl

BaCl2 + 2H2O $$ \to $$ Ba(OH)2 + 2HCl

MgCl2 + 2H2O $$ \to $$ Mg(OH)2 + 2HCl

CaCl2 + 2H2O $$ \to $$ Ca(OH)2 + 2HCl

Be(OH)2 is amphoteric, but the hydroxides of other alkaline earth metals are basic. The basic strength increases down the group. Hence higher the basic character higher will be the pH.

content: The sequence of ionic mobility in aqueous solution is
explanation:
Smaller the size of an ion, greater will be the degree of hydration. So, degree of hydration is highest for Li+ and that is smallest for Cs+. Therefore, Li+ holds the water molecules in its hydration sphere and Cs+ ion holds the least water molecules.

Hence, the correct ionic mobility in aqueous medium is :

Cs+ > Rb+ > K+ > Na+

content: The correct order of increasing thermal stability of K<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>, MgCO<sub>3</sub>, CaCO<sub>3</sub>  and BeCO<sub>3</sub>  is
explanation:
As the basicity of metal hydroxides increases down the group from Be to Ba, the thermal stability of their carbonates also increases in the same order. Further group 1 compounds are more thermally stable than group 2 because their hydroxide are much basic than group 2 hydroxides therefore, the order of thermal stability is

BeCO3 < MgCO3 < CaCO3 < K2CO3

content: In which of the following the hydration energy is higher than the lattice energy?
explanation:
Hydration energy of sulphate decreases on
moving down the group-II. Mg<sup>2+</sup> is smaller than
other ions of group II, so Mg<sup>2+</sup> is readily hydrated
and hence MgSO<sub>4</sub> has higher hydration energy
than the lattice energy.

content: The correct order of the mobility of the alkali metal ions in aqueous solution is
explanation:
Ionic radii of alkali metals in water follows the order
Li+ > Na+ > K+ > Rb+ > Cs+

Thus in aqueous solution due to larger ionic radius Li+ has lowest mobility and hence the correct order of ionic mobility is

Rb+ > K+ > Na+ > Li+

content: A solid compound X on heating gives CO<sub>2</sub> gas and a residue. The residue mixed with water forms Y. On passing an excess of CO<sub>2</sub> through Y in water, a clear solution Z is obtained. On boiling Z, compound X is reformed. The compound X is
explanation: The given compound X must be CaCO3.

content: In which of the following processes, fused sodium hydroxide is electrolysed at a 330<sup>o</sup>C temperature for extraction of sodium?
explanation:
In Castner’s process, for production of
sodium metal, sodium hydroxide (NaOH) is
electrolysed at temperature 330ºC.
